title: Eye and Vision Disorders
authors: Julius T. Oatts, Alejandra G. de Alba Campomanes & Gil Binenbaum

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Eye and Vision Disorders?
Visual system ของ newborn มี critical window ที่แคบมาก: (1) Congenital cataract ต้องผ่าตัดก่อน 6–8 สัปดาห์ เพื่อป้องกัน irreversible deprivation amblyopia — delay 1 สัปดาห์มีผล; (2) Red reflex ก่อน discharge = mandatory by AAP — leukocoria คือ sign ของ cataract/retinoblastoma/PFV; (3) Gonococcal conjunctivitis → corneal perforation ภายใน hours ถ้าไม่รักษา; (4) ROP: neonatologist มี medicolegal responsibility ในการนัด screening ตรงเวลา — delay แม้แค่ 1 สัปดาห์ = disastrous outcome; (5) Anti-VEGF (bevacizumab) สำหรับ ROP: ไม่ได้รับ FDA approval — ต้อง explicit consent + ระวัง neurodevelopmental toxicity; (6) Retinal hemorrhage ใน infant = AHT จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่ — birth-related RH clear ภายใน 2–4 สัปดาห์.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
ทำ red reflex examination ได้ถูกต้อง และรู้ว่า leukocoria / abnormal reflex → referral เร่งด่วน
รู้ visual development timeline: fovea, optic nerve myelination, color vision, fix-and-follow milestones
แยก differential diagnosis ของ leukocoria ได้ (cataract, retinoblastoma, PFV, coloboma, advanced ROP, Coats disease)
รู้ว่า congenital cataract ต้องผ่าตัดก่อนใดถึง 3 เดือน (ideally ก่อน 6 สัปดาห์) เพื่อป้องกัน irreversible amblyopia
รู้ RB1 gene, epidemiology ของ retinoblastoma, และ presenting signs — leukocoria ↔ strabismus
วินิจฉัยและรักษา neonatal conjunctivitis (gonococcal, chlamydial, HSV) — ไม่รอ ophtho consult ก่อนรักษา gonococcal
รู้ ICROP 3rd edition (2021): zones I–III, stages 0–5, plus disease, aggressive ROP (A-ROP)
รู้ ROP screening criteria (AAP): ≤1500g หรือ ≤30 สัปดาห์ gestational age; first exam 31 weeks PMA หรือ 4 weeks chronological
เข้าใจ ROP treatment: laser ablation (ET-ROP standard of care) vs anti-VEGF (BEAT-ROP) — ข้อดี/ข้อเสีย + safety concerns
รู้ retinal hemorrhage ใน birth vs AHT — timeline การ resolve + pattern ที่แตกต่าง
รู้ NICU responsibility: G-ROP criteria, ROP coordinator role, medicolegal implications
1. Neonatal Eye Examination — Approach, Equipment, and Visual Development
1.1 Red Reflex — Mandatory Before Discharge
AAP recommendation: all neonates have red reflex examination ก่อน discharge จาก newborn nursery; ทำซ้ำทุก well-child visit ต่อไป (Donahue & Baker AAP 2016)
Technique: ห้องมืด; direct ophthalmoscope ขนาด largest white-light circle; ส่องทั้ง 2 ตาพร้อมกัน จาก 18 นิ้ว; ดู symmetry ก่อน จากนั้น inspect แต่ละตา
Normal reflex: red, orange, yellow-orange, or maroon — ขึ้นกับ skin/eye pigmentation; lightly pigmented = light orange-yellow, darkly pigmented = dark red; symmetry ระหว่าง 2 ตา สำคัญกว่าสีที่แน่นอน

⚠️ Abnormal Red Reflex → Immediate Referral
Refer ทันทีถ้า: (1) markedly diminished/dim reflex; (2) white reflex (leukocoria); (3) dark spots in reflex; (4) asymmetric reflex between eyes. Exception: transient tear-film opacity ที่ disappear กับ blinking. Direct ophthalmoscope ไม่เหมาะสำหรับ peripheral retinal evaluation (field of view = size of optic nerve head เท่านั้น) — ต้องใช้ indirect ophthalmoscope สำหรับ ROP / retinoblastoma / retinal hemorrhage.
1.2 Visual Development Timeline

rows: Pupillary light reflex onset (not fully mature until 1 month)
Absent pupil reflex < 30 weeks is normal
Head turning to diffuse light starts
Light aversion; visual fixation intermittent; color vision immature
Fix on and follow face/object; color vision greatly improves
Absence of fixation by 2 months → urgent ophtho eval
Blink in response to approaching object (visual threat response)
Absent = suspected visual or neurologic deficit
Optic nerve myelination complete; visual acuity near adult by 2 years
Cortical visual pathway matures throughout first decade

blocks.text: Eyeball at birth: 70% adult size (axial length 17 mm); reaches 95% adult size by 3 years; corneal diameter 9.5 mm at birth (80% adult)

Absence of visual responsiveness by corrected age 2 months → urgent ophthalmologic evaluation; poor vision signs: absent/paradoxical pupillary response, nystagmus/roving eye movements (not before 2–3 months), constant eye poking/rubbing
1.3 Dilation and Exam Techniques

Mydriatic of choice in preterm: Cyclomydril (cyclopentolate 0.2% + phenylephrine 1%) หรือ tropicamide 1% + phenylephrine 2.5%, 3 drops 5 min apart, 30–60 min before exam; ห้ามใช้ cyclopentolate 1% ใน < 6 months; compress lacrimal sac 1–2 min post-instillation เพื่อลด systemic absorption
Side effects ของ mydriatics: ↑ BP, tachycardia, feeding intolerance, gastric emptying delay, urticaria, seizures; bradycardia/apnea = pain response (not direct drug effect)
Pain management during ROP exam: sucrose + non-nutritive sucking + swaddling/nesting + topical anesthetic (proparacaine 0.5% หรือ tetracaine 0.5%) — repeat topical anesthetic → corneal ulceration
Oculocardiac reflex: dysrhythmia (usually bradycardia) จาก direct eye manipulation — ต้องมี assistant monitor during/after exam
2. Leukocoria and Abnormal Red Reflex — Differential Diagnosis

Leukocoria ("white pupil") = spectrum of vision- and life-threatening conditions → urgent ophthalmologic evaluation. พ่อแม่มักพบก่อน (90% ของ retinoblastoma cases เห็น leukocoria ใน recreational photos ก่อน MD ค้นพบ — Abramson Pediatrics 2003).

rows: Opacity in lens; dense central > 3mm = visually significant; may be unilateral or bilateral
Surgery ideally before 6 weeks; maximum by 3 months
Most common ocular malignancy childhood; 1:14,000–18,000; RB1 13q14; leukocoria 54%, strabismus 19%
Immediate referral; genetic testing all patients; 5-year survival > 90% if intraocular
Hyaloid vasculature fails to regress; microphthalmic eye; abnormal red reflex
Surgical intervention to prevent glaucoma/phthisis; most common Rb-simulating lesion
Optic fissure closure failure; inferonasal quadrant; iris/retina/choroid/ON; CHARGE assoc.
Kidney US + audiometry + spine X-ray; echo ± neuroimaging
Retinal detachment; premature infant; bilateral white reflex
Emergency: immediate treatment before retinal detachment progresses
Poor red reflex; birth trauma or AHT
Dilated fundus examination; rule out AHT
Exudative retinal disease; older infants; unilateral

blocks.text: ใน one series (tertiary referral center, n=71): 60% cataracts + 28% retinoblastoma + 12% other retinal abnormalities (Haider J Pediatr Ophthalmol Strabismus 2008) — retinoblastoma must always be ruled out
3. Congenital Cataracts
3.1 Epidemiology และ Visually Significant Threshold
Incidence: 2–4 in 10,000 live births; top 3 cause of preventable childhood blindness; ~10% of childhood blindness worldwide; hereditary cataracts = 22% (Li J Exp Eye Res 2020)
Visually significant threshold: dense central opacity > 3 mm; smaller/nonprogressive cataracts may be observed; nystagmus before surgery = ominous sign of poor visual outcome
Genetics: most common autosomal dominant; ± AR or X-linked recessive; >100 causative genes for lens development; hereditary = 22% of global childhood cataracts
3.2 Etiology — Systemic Associations
Metabolic: galactosemia ("oil-droplet cataracts" — classic, posterior nuclear), neonatal hypoglycemia, hypoparathyroidism, Lowe syndrome
Chromosomal: trisomy 21, Turner syndrome, trisomies 13 and 18
Intrauterine infections: rubella (50% have ocular findings; bilateral in 70%), toxoplasmosis, CMV, varicella, HSV
Connective tissue: Marfan (lens subluxation later, not at birth), Conradi (stippling + cataracts)
Workup for bilateral cataracts: TORCH titers (including syphilis), urine reducing substance (galactosemia), RBC enzyme (galactokinase, GALT), calcium/phosphate/glucose/amino acids, chromosomes + NGS
3.3 Treatment — Time Is Vision

⚠️ Timing Is Everything — Cataract Surgery Window
Useful vision can be restored if surgery completed within 6 weeks after birth. Beyond this, visual restoration progressively more difficult due to irreversible deprivation amblyopia (Birch JAAPOS 2009; Lundvall Acta Ophthalmol 2002). Maximum window: by 3 months of age. Nystagmus appearing before surgery = poor visual prognosis — even greater urgency. Both surgeon AND ophthalmologist at timely referral are the neonatologist's responsibility.
Post-surgical: optical correction (contact lens or glasses) + patching therapy for amblyopia — พ่อแม่ต้องเข้าใจว่า surgery is just the beginning; amblyopia therapy ต่อเนื่องหลายปี
4. Retinoblastoma
Most common ocular malignancy of childhood: 3% of all childhood cancers; incidence 1 in 14,000–18,000 live births in US/Europe; 25% bilateral; 75% unilateral

Genetics: RB1 tumor suppressor gene (first described), chromosome 13q14; Knudson two-hit hypothesis; 5% bilateral disease = large deletion 13q14 syndrome (facial dysmorphism + skeletal anomalies + intellectual disability); germline mutations → risk of nonocular tumors (osteosarcoma, etc.); genetic testing recommended in ALL patients

Presenting signs (Abramson Pediatrics 2003, n=1831): leukocoria 54% + strabismus 19% + poor vision 4% + family history 5% + red eye 5%; 80% first identified by family/friends (not by MD) — stress routine red reflex testing and parental education about leukocoria in photos

Prognosis: 5-year survival > 90% when intraocular at diagnosis; survival < 50% if disease spread outside eye; untreated: almost uniformly fatal; majority diagnosed < 2 years; 95% < 5 years; earliest prenatal dx: 21 weeks' gestation (Maat-Kievit 1993)
5. Other Important Neonatal Eye Conditions
5.1 Congenital Glaucoma
Optic neuropathy + raised IOP; surgical disease (unlike adult glaucoma) — prompt intervention, often neonatal period; vision loss irreversible
Key signs: corneal clouding + buphthalmos (corneal/eye enlargement) + tearing + blepharospasm + Haab striae (tears in Descemet membrane — horizontal lines in red reflex) + photophobia
Important point: tearing = sign of glaucoma, not just blocked tear duct; systemic associations: Sturge-Weber, neurofibromatosis, Lowe syndrome, congenital rubella, Rubinstein-Taybi
5.2 Persistent Fetal Vasculature (PFV) — formerly PHPV
Hyaloid embryonal vasculature fails to regress → spectrum from persistent pupillary strands → vascular stalk remnant → retrolenticular membrane → retinal disorganization/detachment

Involved eyes typically microphthalmic; abnormal red reflex or leukocoria; surgical intervention may prevent recurrent hemorrhage/glaucoma/phthisis bulbi; most common Rb-simulating lesion (followed by Coats disease, then presumed ocular toxocariasis)
5.3 Corneal Clouding — Differential

Transient: slight cloudiness in normal newborns (clears 1–2 days); common in preterm/SGA; premature < 30 weeks: corneal cloudiness may prevent adequate retinal exam for ROP — usually clears by 30–32 weeks postconceptional

Pathologic causes: congenital glaucoma (most urgent) + anterior segment dysgenesis (sclerocornea, Peters anomaly) + infectious keratitis (herpetic — dendritic ulcers; bacterial) + forceps trauma (vertical/diagonal Descemet breaks) + corneal dystrophies + metabolic (mucopolysaccharidoses — later in life)
Peters anomaly: central corneal opacity + clear cornea peripherally + absent posterior stroma + iris/lens attachments to posterior cornea; broader term: anterior segment dysgenesis
5.4 Nasolacrimal Duct Obstruction (NLDO)

Incidence: 5–20% of neonates; > 90% resolve by 12 months; failure = canalization at valve of Hasner (distal end); bilateral in 1/3; epiphora (tearing) + mucopurulent discharge + crusting + white eye — แยกจาก conjunctivitis ด้วย white/quiet eye

Management: digital massage downward from lacrimal sac (empties sac, reduces bacterial growth) + topical antibiotics ถ้า conjunctival injection; refer to ophtho ปลายปีที่ 1 ถ้าไม่ resolve → probing/intubation

⚠️ Dacryocystocele — Potentially Life-Threatening
Dacryocystocele: proximal + distal obstruction → distended lacrimal sac → bluish nontender mass medial/inferior to medial canthus. Serious complications: meningitis + septicemia. IV antibiotics ทันที + decompression after 24–48h ถ้า dacryocystitis ไม่ improve. แยกจาก frontal encephalocele, hemangioma, dermoid cyst.
5.5 Ptosis และ Eyelid Hemangioma
Congenital ptosis: isolated levator muscle anomaly most common; ± third nerve palsy, Horner syndrome, blepharophimosis, Marcus Gunn jaw-winking

Capillary hemangioma: not present at birth; appears first weeks; grows 6–8 months then involutes; amblyogenic (mechanical ptosis + visual axis obstruction + astigmatism); first-line: systemic propranolol (Léauté-Labrèze NEJM 2008/2015 — RCT); topical timolol for superficial lesions

Port wine stain (capillary vascular malformation) — present at birth, no regression; Sturge-Weber syndrome: eyelid involvement → ophtho consult to rule out glaucoma (50%) + choroidal vascular anomalies
6. Ophthalmia Neonatorum — Neonatal Conjunctivitis

Neonatal conjunctivitis = any infection first 4 weeks of life; ± transmitted during delivery or ascend in utero (even after C-section, particularly with prolonged ROM but even intact membranes). Incidence ↓ dramatically since prophylaxis 1881 but still blinds thousands annually worldwide.

rows: Bilateral hyperacute PURULENT; marked lid edema; copious discharge; CAN PERFORATE CORNEA
Cell culture + Gram stain (gram-neg intracellular diplococci)
Ceftriaxone 25–50 mg/kg/day IV + topical irrigation (topical abx only if corneal ulcer)
Mild mucopurulent; nonfollicular; lid edema; pseudomembrane; pneumonitis 3–12 weeks later
Cell culture; Giemsa stain; DFA; ELISA; PCR
Erythromycin 12.5 mg/kg q6h PO × 2 weeks OR azithromycin 20 mg/kg PO × 3 days
Nosocomial; mucoid discharge; conjunctival hyperemia + chemosis
Topical polymyxin B–trimethoprim 1 drop q4h × 7 days
Unilateral/bilateral; serous discharge; lid vesicles; DENDRITIC ulcers (fluorescein stain)
Cell culture; DFA; PCR; multinucleated giant cells
Acyclovir 60 mg/kg/day IV ÷ 3 doses × 2 weeks (3 weeks if CNS/disseminated) + topical trifluridine

blocks.text:
⚠️ Gonococcal Conjunctivitis — Do NOT Wait for Ophthalmology Before Treating
N. gonorrhoeae สามารถ penetrate intact corneal epithelium → globe perforation within hours ถ้าไม่รักษา. ดังนั้น: เก็บ culture ทันทีและเริ่ม ceftriaxone IV โดยไม่ต้องรอ ophthalmology consult. Timing ของ onset ไม่ใช่ reliable diagnostic clue (overlap ระหว่าง agents) — ดังนั้น culture conjunctival scraping สำหรับ Gram + Giemsa stain ทุกราย. Avoid gentamicin ointment — associated with periocular ulcerative dermatitis (Binenbaum J Pediatr 2010).

Prophylaxis agents: 1% silver nitrate, 0.5% erythromycin ointment, 1% tetracycline ointment, 2.5% povidone-iodine; no agent shown to be superior (Kapoor Cochrane 2020); policy should be based on local N. gonorrhoeae + Chlamydia prevalence
7. Retinal Hemorrhages — Birth-Related vs Abusive Head Trauma (AHT)
7.1 Birth-Related Retinal Hemorrhages
Incidence: 10–40% of all newborns; vacuum-assisted delivery: 75–78% (higher due to mechanical compression); C-section: ~7% (Callaway Ophthalmology 2016, Newborn Eye Screen Test study)
Pattern: mostly intraretinal (flame + blot hemorrhages); may extend to periphery; can be bilateral/unilateral/asymmetric; white-centered hemorrhages = nonspecific finding
Timeline: most intraretinal RH clear within 2–3 weeks (many within first few days); ALL intraretinal RH should clear by 4 weeks; preretinal/subretinal may take up to 6 weeks

RH ที่ยังอยู่หลัง 1 เดือน = ไม่ใช่ birth-related
Multiple intraretinal hemorrhages present past 1 month of age → NOT birth-related. Isolated/resolving intraretinal hemorrhage หรือ persistent preretinal/subretinal RH after 1 month would not be considered specific for any cause, รวมถึง abuse.
7.2 Abusive Head Trauma (AHT)
Leading cause of death in infancy; mechanism: repetitive acceleration-deceleration; ± blunt impact; RH present in 50–100% of AHT victims (Binenbaum JAAPOS 2009; Bhardwaj Ophthalmology 2010)

AHT-associated RH pattern: bilateral, too numerous to count, all layers (intraretinal + subretinal + preretinal), extending to ora serrata; macular folds + hemorrhagic macular retinoschisis at severe end
Accidental head trauma: few in number, intraretinal, limited to posterior pole — BUT severe RH + retinal folds can occur with unambiguous severe accidental trauma (motor vehicle crash)

DDx of RH in infancy: birth-related, AHT, coagulopathy, septicemia, leukemia, anemia, glutaric aciduria; CPR: rarely causes RH (few isolated posterior pole intraretinal if any); vaccination/convulsions/coughing = do NOT cause RH (Binenbaum JAMA Ophthalmol 2015)

Ophthalmologic exam within 24–48h of AHT suspicion; dilated fundus exam with indirect ophthalmoscope; document type, number, location, laterality with explicit descriptive terms + diagrams; RetCam photos if available but do NOT substitute for indirect exam
8. Ophthalmic Manifestations of Systemic Diseases — Key Neonatal Associations

rows: Nuclear cataracts, glaucoma, uveitis, microphthalmos, salt-and-pepper retinopathy
50% of rubella have ocular findings; bilateral 70%
Retinitis, vitritis, choroiditis, flat atrophic retinal scars (macular)
75% of congenital toxo have ocular findings; 10% ocular only
Retinochoroiditis, optic nerve atrophy, microphthalmos, cataracts
Most common intrauterine viral infection; < 30% of symptomatic CMV have ophthalmic findings
NLDO, refractive errors, nystagmus, strabismus, cataracts, keratoconus
Refractive errors and strabismus most frequent in NICU
Check GALT enzyme + urine reducing substance; cataract may progress if galactose not eliminated
Foveal hypoplasia, nystagmus, iris transillumination, fundus hypopigmentation, optic nerve misrouting
Nystagmus + decreased acuity; multiple gene types (OCA1–4, X-linked)
Arachnodactyly + lens subluxation (NOT at birth — develops later)
Uveal colobomas (unilateral or bilateral), microphthalmos, Bell palsy
Coloboma = C of CHARGE; renal US + audiometry + spine X-ray
Anophthalmos, cyclopia, microphthalmos, uveal colobomas, cataracts, retinal dysplasia — MOST SEVERE chromosomal eye defects
Most consistently associated with severe ocular defects
Short palpebral fissures, microphthalmos, epicanthal folds, optic nerve hypoplasia, retinal tortuosity

blocks.text: Intrauterine infections table (Table 96.3 Avery): Toxo, syphilis, rubella, CMV, HSV — each has distinct ophthalmic manifestation; syphilis = interstitial keratitis (manifests in childhood, not neonatal period); CMV = most common intrauterine viral infection in US
9. Retinopathy of Prematurity (ROP) — Classification and Screening
9.1 Pathogenesis
ROP = disease of developing retinal vasculature. Two-phase mechanism:

Phase 1 (Vaso-obliteration): premature birth → relative hyperoxia of extrauterine environment + supplemental O2 → damage existing retinal vessels + ↓ VEGF secretion (hypoxia-induced vasoactive molecule for vessel development); ALSO premature birth → loss of maternal IGF-1 (secreted by liver, primarily maternal source until 3rd trimester) → ↓ serum IGF-1 → suppresses VEGF-survival signaling in retinal endothelial cells (Hellstrom PNAS 2001)

Phase 2 (Pathologic Neovascularization): as retinal development progresses → relative hypoxia → when infant's own IGF-1 rises at 30–33 weeks PMA → activates heightened local VEGF → pathologic neovascularization; severity correlates with prematurity degree + hyperoxia + low postnatal IGF-1 levels
9.2 ICROP Third Edition (2021) — International Classification
Reference: Chiang MF, Quinn GE, Fielder AR et al. Ophthalmology 2021;128:e51–68 — most recent classification update

rows: Zone I: most posterior — circle centered on disc, radius = 2× disc-foveal distance (highest risk ROP); Zone II: ring between zone I and nasal ora serrata (most ROP in US occurs here); Zone III: crescent beyond zone II (milder ROP)

Stage 0: incomplete vascularization, no ROP; Stage 1: thin flat white demarcation line; Stage 2: intraretinal elevation (ridge); Stage 3: ridge + EXTRARETINAL neovascular extension into vitreous; Stage 4: partial retinal detachment (4A: fovea spared; 4B: fovea involved); Stage 5: total retinal detachment (5A: open funnel; 5B: closed funnel; 5C: anterior segment involvement)

Spectrum: dilation + tortuosity of posterior pole (Zone I) arterioles AND venules in ≥ 2 quadrants (+ iris engorgement, vitreous haze, pupillary rigidity); Pre-plus: abnormal but insufficient for plus; diagnosis inherently subjective

Rapid neovascularization + severe plus disease without progressing through typical stages; flat neovascularization; AV shunts; ill-defined vascular-avascular junction; previously called 'APROP' (term retired 2021)
30° sectors of ROP along circumference of vascularized retina; NO LONGER used for treatment decisions (ICROP 3rd ed.)

blocks.text: 9.3 ROP Screening Criteria

AAP Screening Criteria (Current US Guidelines)
Screen infants with: (1) birth weight ≤ 1500g; OR (2) gestational age ≤ 30 weeks 0 days; PLUS: (3) infants 1501–2000g at high risk by neonatologist judgment. First examination: 31 weeks PMA OR 4 weeks chronological age — whichever is LATER. Exams q2 weeks unless ROP develops → frequency ↑. ในประเทศรายได้ปานกลาง (Latin America, Asia, E. Europe): สำหรับ larger/older infants ก็ยังมี risk → ต้องใช้ national/regional criteria.

G-ROP criteria (Postnatal Growth and ROP Study Group): ≥ 1 of: GA < 28 weeks; BW < 1051g; weight gain < 120g/180g/170g during days 10–19/20–29/30–39; hydrocephalus; maintained 100% sensitivity + reduced 30% infants requiring exams + prospectively validated (n≈4,000) (Quinn JAMA Ophthalmol 2018; G-ROP Study Group) + estimated > 36,000 fewer exams/year, annual cost savings ~ $3M USD

Current limitations of existing guidelines: low specificity (only 5–7% need treatment); GA + BW alone miss some cases → subjective 3rd criterion ("poor postnatal course") still needed; G-ROP criteria not yet formally adopted in AAP guidelines
10. ROP Treatment — Laser Ablation vs Anti-VEGF
10.1 Treatment Threshold — ET-ROP

Current standard: treat at Type 1 ROP (ET-ROP threshold): (a) any stage with plus in Zone I; (b) stage 3 without plus in Zone I; (c) stage 2 or 3 with plus in Zone II; ไม่ต้องรอ 'threshold' (clock hours) ตาม CRYO-ROP อีกต่อไป (Early Treatment for ROP Cooperative Group Arch Ophthalmol 2003)
Treatment: within 72 hours ของ meeting criteria; delay แม้แค่ 1 สัปดาห์ = significant risk of progression → neonatologist ต้องเน้น urgency กับพ่อแม่
10.2 Laser Photocoagulation — Standard of Care

Mechanism: ablates peripheral avascular retina (source of VEGF) → ↓ VEGF signaling → prevents pathologic neovascularization; full effect takes 1 week or more (free VEGF must be broken down after avascular retina destroyed)
Preferred over cryotherapy: less pain, direct visualization; for stages 4–5 (retinal detachment): results mixed + much less successful; total RD (stage 5): good functional visual outcome unlikely
Long-term concern: high myopia — especially Zone I eyes treated with laser (> 36% Zone I eyes at 6-year ET-ROP follow-up had high myopia + > 30% had VA 20/200 or worse)
10.3 Anti-VEGF Treatment — BEAT-ROP and Beyond

BEAT-ROP (Mintz-Hittner NEJM 2011): multicenter RCT — bevacizumab (intravitreal) in Zone I and posterior Zone II type 1 ROP; retreatment rate 6% (bevacizumab) vs 42% (laser) in Zone I subgroup by 54 weeks PMA
Mechanism: binds free VEGF directly → effect within 12–24h (vs laser: 1 week); advantage = speed of action for aggressive/posterior ROP

Refractive advantage: bevacizumab → significantly LOWER incidence of high myopia at 2.5 years vs laser (3.8% Zone I + 1.7% Zone II bevacizumab vs 36.4% Zone I + 51.4% Zone II laser, p < 0.001) (BEAT-ROP 2013)

⚠️ Anti-VEGF Safety Concerns — Critical Discussion Points
(1) Recurrence: ROP may recur up to 2–3 years after anti-VEGF (VEGF source not destroyed) → extended follow-up required; many ultimately need laser if vascularization not complete by 50–60 weeks PMA. (2) Systemic VEGF suppression: significant systemic absorption → serum VEGF ↓ for ≥ 60 days → concerns about neurodevelopment, lung, kidney vasculogenesis. (3) Neurodevelopmental risk: Canadian + Taiwan observational studies — bevacizumab infants 2–3× more likely unfavorable developmental outcomes at 18 months vs laser (Marlow/Kong 2019); confounding by indication may be a factor. (4) Off-label: no anti-VEGF FDA approved for ROP → explicit consent required. ต้องอธิบายทั้งหมดนี้กับพ่อแม่ก่อนเลือก anti-VEGF.
10.4 ROP Prevention

SUPPORT/COT/BOOST II meta-analysis: SpO2 85–89% vs 91–95% — no significant difference in severe ROP (RR 0.72, 95% CI 0.50–1.04); HOWEVER: ↑ NEC + ↑ death before discharge in restricted oxygen group → still uncertainty about optimal SpO2 target range

Under investigation: Vitamin E, omega-3 PUFA, systemic/topical beta-blockers, IV IGF-1 + IGFBP — none proven; slow postnatal weight gain = reliable surrogate for ↓ IGF-1 → foundation for G-ROP predictive models
11. NICU Practical — Eye Care Responsibilities
Red reflex ก่อน discharge ทุกราย: leukocoria / dim / asymmetric → same-day/urgent referral to pediatric ophthalmologist; ไม่รอ routine follow-up

Congenital cataract: refer immediately for surgery consideration (เป้าหมาย < 6 สัปดาห์); bilateral workup: TORCH + metabolic (galactosemia, Lowe) + chromosomes/NGS; nystagmus = poor prognostic sign — even greater urgency

ROP scheduling — medicolegal responsibility: schedule all exams before discharge; เน้นกับพ่อแม่: delay 1 สัปดาห์ = potential blindness; ROP coordinator ช่วยประสาน inpatient + outpatient follow-up; อย่า cancel exam unilaterally ถ้า unstable — คุยกับ ophthalmologist ก่อน

Gonococcal conjunctivitis: เริ่ม ceftriaxone IV + conjunctival culture + Gram stain ทันที — ไม่รอ ophthalmology; topical irrigation; Chlamydia: erythromycin PO × 2 weeks (ลด pneumonitis risk); HSV: acyclovir IV × 2–3 weeks; ห้ามใช้ gentamicin ointment (periocular ulceration)

AHT suspected: ophthalmologic exam within 24–48h (RH begins to resolve early); dilated indirect fundus exam + detailed documentation + RetCam photos ถ้าทำได้; ความ RH + AHT: ↑ RH severity → ↑ likelihood of abuse (Binenbaum JAAPOS 2009)
Dacryocystocele: IV antibiotics ทันที → ถ้า dacryocystitis ไม่ respond 24–48h → surgical decompression; แยกจาก encephalocele + hemangioma; risk meningitis/septicemia
Premature infant eyelid hemangioma: propranolol เป็น first-line ถ้า amblyogenic — follow visual axis + red reflex closely; port wine stain + eyelid involvement → ophtho consult + IOP monitoring

Infection control สำหรับ ROP exams: disinfect indirect ophthalmoscope + lens ระหว่าง patients (bleach → rinse water); adenoviral outbreak in NICU traced to ROP exam equipment — standard + isolation precautions + hand hygiene essential
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Leukocoria ใน Photo = Emergency Until Proven Otherwise
80% ของ retinoblastoma ถูกค้นพบโดยพ่อแม่/เพื่อนก่อนแพทย์ — ส่วนใหญ่จาก recreational photos. Leukocoria ใน photo = referral เร่งด่วน. DDx: cataract (60%), retinoblastoma (28%), other (12%) ใน tertiary series. Untreated retinoblastoma = almost uniformly fatal; 5-year survival > 90% ถ้า intraocular at diagnosis. ดังนั้น red reflex ทุก well-child visit + สอนพ่อแม่ให้ watch for white pupil ในรูปถ่าย.

PEARL 2 — Cataract Surgery Timing: 6 Weeks Is the Window
Visually significant cataract (dense central > 3mm) → surgery before 6 weeks (ideally); maximum window: before 3 months. After this, irreversible deprivation amblyopia. Nystagmus appearing before surgery = ominous sign. Refer ทันทีที่สงสัย — ไม่รอ 1–2 สัปดาห์. Post-surgery: contact lens + patching therapy for amblyopia ต้องต่อเนื่อง — surgery เป็นแค่จุดเริ่มต้น.

PEARL 3 — Gonococcal Conjunctivitis: Hours Matter
N. gonorrhoeae penetrates intact corneal epithelium → globe perforation within hours. ดังนั้น: เก็บ culture + เริ่ม ceftriaxone IV ทันที — ไม่รอ ophthalmology consult. Onset timing ไม่ reliable (overlap ระหว่าง agents). ห้ามใช้ gentamicin ointment (periocular ulceration). Timing ของ prophylaxis (erythromycin/povidone-iodine/silver nitrate): ไม่มี agent ที่ดีกว่าอีก agent — เลือกตาม local epidemiology.

PEARL 4 — ROP: Neonatologist มี Medicolegal Responsibility ในการนัด Follow-up
ROP claims = 0.5–4% ของ ophthalmology lawsuits — causes: inappropriately long intervals, failure of outpatient referral, unsupervised care. Schedule all outpatient appointments before discharge; เน้นกับพ่อแม่ว่า delay 1 สัปดาห์ = potential blindness; ถ้า unstable → คุยกับ ophthalmologist อย่า cancel unilaterally. G-ROP criteria รักษา 100% sensitivity + ลด unnecessary exams 30% (Quinn 2018).

PEARL 5 — Anti-VEGF ≠ Risk-Free ROP Cure
Bevacizumab/anti-VEGF สำหรับ ROP: faster action, less myopia vs laser แต่: (1) recurrence up to 2–3 years — extended follow-up mandatory; (2) systemic VEGF suppression ≥ 60 days — concern for brain/lung/kidney development; (3) observational data suggest 2–3× unfavorable neurodevelopment at 18 months vs laser (confounding by indication unclear); (4) ไม่มี FDA approval — explicit consent ต้องมี. Laser = still gold standard เว้นแต่ aggressive/posterior disease.
13. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Donahue SP, Baker CN. AAP Pediatrics 2016;137:902: Visual system assessment by pediatricians — red reflex examination mandatory at birth and all subsequent well-child visits; any abnormality/asymmetry → referral.

Abramson DH et al. Pediatrics 2003;112:1248 (n=1831 retinoblastoma): Leukocoria 54% + strabismus 19% as presenting signs; 80% first identified by family/friend — stresses pediatric red reflex screening.
Birch EE et al. JAAPOS 2009;13:67: Critical period for dense bilateral congenital cataract surgery — outcomes progressively worse after early months; surgery before 6 weeks = best visual prognosis.

Kapoor VS, Evans JR, Vedula SS. Cochrane 2020(9): Interventions for preventing ophthalmia neonatorum — no prophylactic agent shown to have superior effectiveness; local N. gonorrhoeae/Chlamydia prevalence should guide policy.
Binenbaum G et al. JAAPOS 2009;13:268: Odds of abuse associated with retinal hemorrhages — increasing RH severity correlates with increasing likelihood of AHT.
Binenbaum G et al. JAMA Ophthalmol 2015;133:1261: Vaccination injections, convulsions, and severe coughing do NOT cause retinal hemorrhages — important for AHT evaluation.

Callaway NF et al. Ophthalmology 2016;123:1043 (Newborn Eye Screen Test Study): Retinal and optic nerve hemorrhages in newborns — incidence, distribution, natural history; vacuum 75–78%, C-section 7%.

Chiang MF, Quinn GE et al. Ophthalmology 2021;128:e51 (ICROP 3rd Edition): Updated international ROP classification — revised zone II subdivision (posterior zone II), reframes plus disease as spectrum, retires 'APROP' term in favor of 'aggressive ROP (A-ROP)'.

Early Treatment for ROP Cooperative Group. Arch Ophthalmol 2003;121:1684 (ET-ROP): Type 1 ROP criteria for treatment — earlier intervention vs conventional management; current treatment guideline basis; clock hours no longer used for treatment decisions.

Mintz-Hittner HA et al. NEJM 2011;364:603 (BEAT-ROP): Bevacizumab vs laser for zone I and posterior zone II type 1 ROP — bevacizumab: significantly lower retreatment rate in zone I (6% vs 42%). First RCT demonstrating anti-VEGF effectiveness.

Quinn GE et al. JAMA Ophthalmol 2018;136:1383 (G-ROP Study Group): Postnatal Growth and ROP study — new G-ROP screening criteria using postnatal weight gain; 30% reduction in infants requiring exams while maintaining 100% sensitivity; prospectively validated n≈4000.

Léauté-Labrèze C et al. NEJM 2008;358:2649 AND NEJM 2015;372:735: Propranolol for severe infantile hemangioma — first report (2008) then multicenter RCT (2015); oral propranolol now standard first-line treatment for amblyogenic periocular hemangioma.

SUPPORT/COT/BOOST II Meta-analysis 2016 (Saugstad/ILCOR): SpO2 85–89% vs 91–95% in preterm — no significant difference in severe ROP (RR 0.72); higher NEC + death in restricted oxygen group → uncertainty about optimal target range persists.

Hellstrom A et al. PNAS 2001;98:5804: Low IGF-1 suppresses VEGF-survival signaling in retinal endothelial cells — direct correlation with clinical ROP; foundational paper for postnatal weight gain prediction models.
Barry GP et al. Ophthalmology 2020 Dec 31: Lower short-term retinal detachment with anti-VEGF vs laser when treatment before 36 weeks PMA — supporting anti-VEGF for early aggressive ROP.
14. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Newborn มี white reflex ใน left eye ตอน red reflex examination — ต้องทำอะไรต่อไป? differential diagnosis มีอะไรบ้าง?

ตอบ: White reflex = leukocoria → urgent ophthalmologic referral ทันที (same day). DDx ที่สำคัญ: cataract (60% ใน tertiary series), retinoblastoma (28%), PFV, coloboma, advanced ROP, vitreous hemorrhage, Coats disease. ห้ามรอ routine referral เพราะ retinoblastoma = life-threatening + cataract = amblyopia ถ้า delay. Retinoblastoma: RB1 tumor suppressor gene 13q14; 5-year survival > 90% if intraocular → delay in diagnosis correlates directly with worse stage.
Q: Preterm 27 สัปดาห์ BW 800g — เมื่อไหร่ควรทำ first ROP exam? ใช้ criteria อะไร?

ตอบ: AAP criteria: BW ≤ 1500g AND GA ≤ 30 สัปดาห์ → first exam at 31 weeks PMA OR 4 weeks chronological (whichever is LATER). ทารกนี้ GA 27 สัปดาห์ → first exam: 31 weeks PMA = 4 weeks corrected age (31–27=4 weeks), which in this case aligns at 4 weeks chronological. Continue q2 weeks unless ROP develops. G-ROP criteria: GA < 28 weeks → meet criteria automatically. Medicolegal: schedule outpatient follow-up before discharge; emphasize to parents that 1-week delay = potential blindness.
Q: Newborn 3 days มี copious purulent conjunctivitis + marked lid edema — diagnosis? management ทันที?

ตอบ: Gonococcal conjunctivitis ตาม onset (3–4 days) + presentation (hyperacute purulent + lid edema). เก็บ conjunctival culture (Thayer-Martin agar) + Gram + Giemsa stain ทันที; เริ่ม ceftriaxone 25–50 mg/kg IV + topical irrigation ทันที — ไม่รอ ophthalmology consult. Rationale: N. gonorrhoeae penetrates intact corneal epithelium → globe perforation within hours. Topical antibiotics added only if corneal ulcer present. ห้ามใช้ gentamicin ointment. Notify ophthalmology for corneal/intraocular evaluation. Evaluate mother for gonorrhea + treat.
Q: ICROP 3rd edition: อธิบาย Type 1 ROP ที่ต้องรักษา และทำไม anti-VEGF บางครั้งดีกว่า laser สำหรับ Zone I?

ตอบ: Type 1 ROP (ET-ROP criteria — treatment threshold): (a) any stage + plus disease ใน Zone I; (b) stage 3 without plus ใน Zone I; (c) stage 2 or 3 + plus disease ใน Zone II. Treat within 72 hours. Anti-VEGF advantage for Zone I: speed of action (12–24h vs 1 week for laser) — anti-VEGF binds free VEGF directly; laser must destroy avascular retina → free VEGF gradually broken down. For aggressive/early-PMA ROP where speed critical: Barry 2020 showed lower short-term retinal detachment with anti-VEGF before 36 weeks PMA. But: recurrence risk up to 2–3 years + systemic VEGF suppression + no FDA approval — explicit consent required.
Q: Birth-related retinal hemorrhage vs AHT — timeline + pattern ที่ช่วย distinguish?

ตอบ: Birth-related RH: intraretinal (flame/blot), clear within 2–3 weeks (all clear by 4 weeks); preretinal/subretinal up to 6 weeks; vacuum delivery: 75–78% incidence. AHT-associated RH: bilateral too-numerous-to-count, all layers, extends to ora serrata, macular folds/retinoschisis; ± unilateral or markedly asymmetric; usually more severe. Key rule: multiple intraretinal RH past 1 month = NOT birth-related. CPR, vaccination, convulsions, coughing = do NOT cause RH. Exam within 24–48h (RH begins to resolve early) + detailed documentation with diagrams.
Q: Congenital nasolacrimal duct obstruction (NLDO): natural history + management + เมื่อไหร่ refer?

ตอบ: 5–20% of neonates; > 90% resolve spontaneously by 12 months; failure at distal end (valve of Hasner); presents 1 month (80% of cases): epiphora + discharge + crusting + WHITE quiet eye (แยกจาก conjunctivitis). Management: digital massage downward over nasolacrimal sac (empties sac, ↓ bacteria) + topical antibiotics ถ้า conjunctival injection. Refer to ophthalmologist at end of year 1 ถ้าไม่ resolve → probing ± intubation. Dacryocystocele ≠ simple NLDO: bluish mass medial canthus → IV antibiotics ทันที (meningitis/sepsis risk).
Q: Periocular capillary hemangioma — เมื่อไหร่ต้องรักษา? treatment ที่ prefer?

ตอบ: รักษาถ้า amblyogenic: (1) mechanical ptosis obstructing visual axis; (2) significant astigmatism (corneal compression); (3) direct occlusion of pupil. Hemangioma: ไม่มีที่ birth — เริ่ม first weeks; grows 6–8 months then involutes. First-line: oral propranolol (systemic beta-blocker) — remarkable regression (Léauté-Labrèze NEJM 2008 + RCT 2015); topical timolol for superficial lesions. Other options: steroids (intralesional/systemic), vincristine, surgery (reserved). แยกจาก port wine stain (present at birth, no regression) → Sturge-Weber → glaucoma in 50%.
Q: Congenital coloboma — อธิบาย embryology, location, systemic workup?

ตอบ: Embryology: failure of optic fissure closure during embryogenesis → area of missing tissue; most commonly inferonasal quadrant; incidence 0.5–7.5 per 10,000 births. Structures involved: iris (keyhole/cat's-eye pupil), ciliary body, retina, choroid, optic nerve (coloboma → leukocoria or abnormal red reflex). Visual prognosis depends on whether macula is involved. Systemic workup for apparently isolated uveal coloboma: kidney US + audiometry + spine radiographs + consider echo + neuroimaging (Nakamura Arch Ophthalmol 2011; Huynh Am J Ophthalmol 2013). Associations: CHARGE syndrome (CHD7), 22q11 deletion, Treacher Collins, Walker-Warburg, Aicardi syndrome.
Day 97 Preview

Avery Chapter 97 — Hearing and Auditory Disorders in the Newborn
พรุ่งนี้: Hearing and Auditory Disorders — Part XX Eyes and Ears ต่อเนื่อง: จาก visual pathway (Day 96) มาสู่ auditory pathway และ congenital hearing loss. Topics ที่จะครอบคลุม: universal newborn hearing screening, auditory brainstem response (ABR) vs OAE (otoacoustic emissions), risk factors for sensorineural hearing loss ใน NICU (ototoxic drugs, meningitis, hyperbilirubinemia, CMV), JCIH 2019 guidelines, cochlear implant timing, และ early intervention — critical window สำหรับ speech/language development มีความสำคัญเช่นเดียวกับ visual amblyopia.
